negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing dedicated node-managed Scheduled subpool with retrieval and hash pruning that avoids ordinary sender accounting.
related_but_insufficient_code: The port has no pool/scheduled.rs file. Pool insertion only detects SCHEDULED_TX_TYPE_ID to avoid async sender lock, then uses normal TxPool insertion.

rationale: The pool-accounting lineage moved from keyed maps, scheduled TxState bits, and dedicated scheduled error variants to a dedicated node-managed Scheduled subpool. Later candidates consistently preserve direct scheduled-subpool insertion/retrieval and hash removal while removing normal insert_tx scheduled classification.
statement: Node-generated scheduled transactions are stored and retrieved through the dedicated Scheduled subpool; normal transaction insertion no longer classifies transactions with scheduled TxState bits or scheduled-specific invalid-pool errors, and hash-based pruning must check and remove scheduled-subpool transactions without polluting ordinary sender accounting.
suggested_falsification_tests: Insert a node-generated scheduled transaction through the scheduled insertion path, assert get_scheduled_transactions returns it from the Scheduled subpool, remove it by hash, and assert it is absent from the Scheduled subpool afterward.

Insert otherwise similar scheduled and non-scheduled transactions through normal insert_tx and assert no SCHEDULED_TRANSACTION TxState bit or ScheduledPoolTransactionError path is required or exposed.
Populate ordinary transaction accounting with SYSTEM_SENDER and a normal sender, then assert unique_senders excludes SYSTEM_SENDER.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert node-generated scheduled tx through scheduled insertion path and retrieve/remove it from Scheduled subpool; current port lacks this path.
title: Scheduled transactions use a dedicated node-managed subpool
